sections.paragraph:
ἡδέως adv. of ἡδύς (Soph., Hippocr.+) pert. to being pleased in connection with someth., gladly (Aristoph., Thu. et al.) λαμβάνειν (Aristoph., Equ. 440) ITr 6:2. ἡδ. ἀκούειν like to hear (Jos., Ant. 3, 191; Just., D. 10, 4; 59, 1) Mk 6:20; 12:37; Hm 2:2. ἀνέχεσθαι gladly tolerate 2 Cor 11:19. ἡδ. ποιεῖν (Ps.-Menand., Dist. Fgm. 704 Kock; POxy 113, 30; PGrenf II, 73, 20) do gladly Hs 6, 5, 5. βαστάζειν 8, 10, 3; 9, 14, 6. ὑποδέχεσθαι 8, 10, 3; 9, 27, 2. Ἰσαὰκ ἡδ. προσήγετο θυσία. I. gladly let himself be led as a sacrifice 1 Cl 31:3. ὸ̔ν καὶ ὁ βασιλεὺς ἡδέως ἔχει (the child) of whom even Caesar is fond Ox 849, 16f. Restored text: [ἡδέως θλείβον]ται Hs 8, 10, 4f (s. Bonner 113, 18f, based on Lat. libenter patiuntur).—Comp. ἥδιον (Lysias 7, 40; IPriene 105, 19 [c. 9 b.c.]; BGU 372 I, 15; Sir 22:11; Just., D. 29, 1) more gladly ἥδ. διδόναι 1 Cl 2:1 (cp. μακάριος 2bγ). ὑπομιμνῄσκειν 62:3. Superl. as elative (B-D-F §60, 2; Rob. 278f; 670; M-M s.v.) ἥδιστα very gladly (Pla., Theaet. 183d; Lucian, Scyth. 8; POxy 1061, 21 [22 b.c.]; 933, 5; PLond III, 897, 8 p. 207 [84 a.d.]; Jos., Vi. 365) 2 Cor 12:15; Ac 13:8 D. ἥδιστα μᾶλλον καυχήσομαι I will rather boast all the more gladly 2 Cor 12:9 (s. B-D-F §246).—B. 1032. DELG s.v. ἥδομαι B (ἡδύ). M-M. Spicq.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

someth. someth. = something

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Kock Kock , s. Com.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PGrenf II PGrenf II = BGrenfell/AHunt, New Classical Fragments—Lists 4

Hs Hs = Similitudes

1 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

Bonner Bonner = CB., s. H (=Hermas)—List 1

Lat. Lat. = Latin

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

IPriene IPriene = Die Inschriften von Priene—List 3

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 434
